article_title: China Opens First Mandatory L2 Driver-Assistance Standard to Public Comment
summary: Submission content is reviewable, but the PR branch includes unrelated submissions and cannot be merged as-is

findings.message: PR branch contains unrelated submission files
findings.details: PR #951 is titled and branched for the China L2 ADAS submission, but it also includes src/content/submissions/2026-04/2026-04-19T19-16-45Z_rust-195-adds-cfg_select-extends-if-let-guards-and.json and src/content/submissions/2026-04/2026-04-19T19-16-40Z_india-sets-up-aigeg-to-centralize-ai-governance-an.json. Those belong to other review flows, and the India PR (#958) has already been merged. Merging #951 as-is would create a contaminated merge.

content_preview.title: China Opens First Mandatory L2 Driver-Assistance Standard to Public Comment
content_preview.summary: China has opened a public comment period on a mandatory national standard for L2 driver-assistance systems, signaling a tighter safety baseline for intelligent connected vehicles.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China has opened public comment on what appears to be its first mandatory national standard for Level 2 driver-assistance systems in intelligent connected vehicles, according to [China Daily](https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202604/16/WS69e040e7a310d6866eb43c13.html). The consultation runs from April 16 to April 22, 2026, and the proposed effective date is January 1, 2027, the same report says.

## What We Know

The draft says drivers must remain actively engaged in the driving task ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
Clean the PR branch so it contains only the intended China L2 submission before resubmitting for merge

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is concise, neutral, and correctly separates verified facts from inference. The framing around driver responsibility and lockout behavior is proportionate to the source material.
editor_notes.source_verification: I read all three listed URLs directly. China Daily confirms the public-comment window from April 16 to April 22, 2026, the mandatory-standard framing, the requirement that drivers remain actively engaged, and the proposed January 1, 2027 effective date. News18a supports the passenger-vehicle and goods-vehicle scope plus the hands-off or eyes-off lockout mechanics. ChinaEVHome supports the broad list of participating automakers and suppliers used in the article's industry-group claim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: For the China L2 submission itself, the claims are supported by the listed sources and the analysis is labeled as inference where appropriate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The article does not oversell the draft as higher-autonomy legalization.
editor_notes.originality: Originality check passed for the China L2 topic. No recent Machine Herald article covers this exact standard-setting development.
concerns: The PR is structurally invalid because it contains unrelated submission files in addition to the China L2 article.
One of the unrelated files corresponds to India AIGEG, whose own PR (#958) has already merged, so merging this branch would produce a polluted history.
recommendations: Remove the unrelated Rust and India submission files from PR #951 and keep only the China L2 submission.
Resubmit or update the PR branch, then request re-review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Request changes. The China L2 article itself is reviewable, but the PR cannot be merged until the branch is cleaned up.
